title: Admin Entity Lists v2 — filters, grid cards, host portfolio & tags migration

tasks.title: Extend FilterControlType union and add range config types
tasks.description:
Extend the filter type system with two new discriminated union members.

**Files to modify**:
- `apps/admin/src/components/entity-list/filters/filter-types.ts`

**What to implement**:
1. Change `FilterControlType` from `'select' | 'boolean'` to `'select' | 'boolean' | 'number-range' | 'date-range'`.
2. Add `NumberRangeFilterConfig` interface:
   - `type: 'number-range'`
   - `paramKeyMin: string` (e.g. `'priceMin'`)
   - `paramKeyMax: string` (e.g. `'priceMax'`)
   - `min?: number`, `max?: number`, `step?: number`, `unitLabelKey?: string`
3. Add `DateRangeFilterConfig` interface:
   - `type: 'date-range'`
   - `paramKeyFrom: string` (e.g. `'createdAtFrom'`)
   - `paramKeyTo: string` (e.g. `'createdAtTo'`)
   - Values serialize as ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` strings
4. Update the `FilterConfig` discriminated union to include both new types.
5. Add JSDoc on all new types.

**Acceptance criteria**:
- `FilterControlType` includes `'number-range'` and `'date-range'`
- Both new config interfaces are exported and JSDoc-documented
- Existing `SelectFilterConfig` and `BooleanFilterConfig` shapes are unchanged
- `pnpm typecheck` passes in apps/admin
- No existing list breaks (purely additive change)

subtasks.title: Change FilterControlType union to include 'number-range' and 'date-range'
Add NumberRangeFilterConfig interface with paramKeyMin/paramKeyMax + optional bounds
Add DateRangeFilterConfig interface with paramKeyFrom/paramKeyTo
Update FilterConfig discriminated union to include new types
Verify pnpm typecheck passes — additive, no breakage

tasks.title: Build FilterNumberRange and FilterDateRange UI controls
tasks.description:
Implement the two new filter control React components and register them in FilterBar.

**Files to create**:
- `apps/admin/src/components/entity-list/filters/FilterNumberRange.tsx`
- `apps/admin/src/components/entity-list/filters/FilterDateRange.tsx`

**Files to modify**:
- `apps/admin/src/components/entity-list/filters/FilterBar.tsx`

**FilterNumberRange.tsx**:
- Receives `NumberRangeFilterConfig` + current min/max values from filter state
- Two `<input type="number">` fields (min, max), each independently clearable
- Shows `unitLabelKey` label when provided (i18n-resolved)
- Respects optional `min`/`max`/`step` props as HTML input attributes
- On change: calls the state updater with the new bound value (empty = clear = sentinel)
- Tailwind v4 styling (no CSS modules); Shadcn Input component for field styling

**FilterDateRange.tsx**:
- Receives `DateRangeFilterConfig` + current from/to values from filter state
- Two `<input type="date">` fields (from, to), each independently clearable
- Values formatted as `YYYY-MM-DD` (HTML date input native format)
- On change: calls the state updater with the date string value
- Tailwind v4 styling; Shadcn Input for field styling

**FilterBar.tsx registration**:
- Add `case 'number-range'`: render `<FilterNumberRange>`
- Add `case 'date-range'`: render `<FilterDateRange>`
- Preserve existing `select` and `boolean` cases unchanged

**Acceptance criteria**:
- Both components render without console errors
- Clearing a bound reverts to the sentinel model (independent per bound)
- `pnpm typecheck` passes in apps/admin
- Tests alongside: render test for each component (value set, clear, apply)
- Tailwind v4 only — no CSS modules

subtasks.title: Create FilterNumberRange.tsx with dual independent number inputs
Create FilterDateRange.tsx with dual independent date inputs
Register both controls in FilterBar.tsx switch/case
Write render + apply + clear tests for FilterNumberRange
Write render + apply + clear tests for FilterDateRange

tasks.title: Extend useFilterState for two-param range URL serialization
tasks.description:
Extend the filter state hook to handle the new range filter types, each serializing as two independent URL params using the existing `FILTER_CLEARED_SENTINEL` 3-state model.

**Files to modify**:
- `apps/admin/src/components/entity-list/filters/useFilterState.ts`

**What to implement**:
1. For `number-range` filters: serialize `paramKeyMin` and `paramKeyMax` as two independent URL params.
   - Each bound can be set, cleared (sentinel), or absent (default/never touched).
   - When a bound is absent from the query, omit it from the API request.
   - When a bound is set to sentinel, clear it from the URL.
2. For `date-range` filters: same pattern using `paramKeyFrom` and `paramKeyTo`.
3. The existing serialization for `select` and `boolean` filters MUST be unchanged.
4. The active-filter chip logic must render one chip per populated bound (so each bound is individually clearable).

**Acceptance criteria**:
- URL gains `priceMin=1000&priceMax=5000` when both bounds are set
- URL gains `priceMin=1000` only when max is absent
- Clearing one bound removes only its URL param (the other persists)
- Existing `select` + `boolean` serialization is untouched (regression tests pass)
- Tests: at minimum 6 unit tests — set-both, set-min-only, set-max-only, clear-min, clear-max, clear-both

subtasks.title: Add number-range serialization logic (paramKeyMin / paramKeyMax) to useFilterState
Add date-range serialization logic (paramKeyFrom / paramKeyTo) to useFilterState
Update active-filter chip logic so each bound is independently clearable
Write 6 unit tests: set-both, min-only, max-only, clear-min, clear-max, clear-both
Verify existing select + boolean serialization tests still pass

tasks.title: Extend createEntityApi to forward range bounds and wire backend range filters
tasks.description:
Wire the range params through the full vertical slice: API client forwards bounds, backend schemas declare them, services apply them.

**Files to modify**:
- `apps/admin/src/components/entity-list/api/createEntityApi.ts`
- Relevant `packages/schemas/` admin list query schemas (accommodations, events, destinations)
- Relevant `packages/service-core/` service filter methods

**createEntityApi.ts**:
- For each `number-range` filter config: include `paramKeyMin` and `paramKeyMax` in the request only when their values are present and non-sentinel.
- For each `date-range` filter config: include `paramKeyFrom` and `paramKeyTo` the same way.
- Existing `paramKey` forwarding for `select`/`boolean` is untouched.

**Backend Zod schemas** (in `packages/schemas/`):
- Add `priceMin?: number`, `priceMax?: number` to accommodations admin list schema.
- Add `ratingMin?: number`, `ratingMax?: number` to accommodations admin list schema (rating filter).
- Add `createdAtFrom?: string`, `createdAtTo?: string` to accommodations, destinations, and events list schemas.
- Add `startDateFrom?: string`, `startDateTo?: string` to events list schema for event date filtering.
- All new params are optional and declared explicitly so `createAdminListRoute` accepts them.

**service-core service filters**:
- AccommodationService.adminList: apply `WHERE price >= priceMin AND price <= priceMax` (centavo integers), `WHERE rating >= ratingMin AND rating <= ratingMax`, `WHERE created_at >= createdAtFrom AND created_at <= createdAtTo`.
- EventService.adminList: apply `createdAt` range and `startDate` range filters.
- DestinationService.adminList: apply `createdAt` range filter.
- Each bound is optional: present → apply; absent → skip.

**Acceptance criteria**:
- `createEntityApi` only sends a bound param when it has a non-empty, non-sentinel value
- Admin list route rejects an undeclared range param (proof: add a test with an unknown param)
- Service tests: price range inclusive both ends, min-only, max-only, date range inclusive, single date bound
- `pnpm typecheck` passes in apps/admin, packages/schemas, packages/service-core

subtasks.title: Extend createEntityApi.ts to forward range bound params when present
Add price/rating range params to accommodations admin list Zod schema
Add createdAt range params to accommodations, destinations, events Zod schemas
Add startDate range params to events Zod schema
Wire price/rating/date range filters into AccommodationService.adminList
Wire date/startDate range filters into EventService.adminList and DestinationService.adminList
Write service tests: boundary cases (inclusive min/max, single bound, empty, inverted range guard)

tasks.title: Wire number-range and date-range filters into entity configs
tasks.description:
Add the new range filter types to the entity configs that should expose them.

**Files to modify** (config files under `apps/admin/src/features/*/config/`):
- `accommodations/config/accommodations.config.ts` — add `priceMin`/`priceMax` number-range + `createdAtFrom`/`createdAtTo` date-range
- `events/config/events.config.ts` — add `startDateFrom`/`startDateTo` date-range + `createdAtFrom`/`createdAtTo` date-range
- `destinations/config/destinations.config.ts` — add `createdAtFrom`/`createdAtTo` date-range

**For each config**, extend the `filterBarConfig.filters` array with the appropriate `NumberRangeFilterConfig` or `DateRangeFilterConfig` entries matching the param keys declared in T-004 backend schemas.

**Note**: `number-range` on money columns (price) operates on stored integer centavos. Add `unitLabelKey` pointing to an i18n key like `'filters.unit.ars'` or similar so the UI can label the unit. If no i18n key exists, add a minimal one to `@repo/i18n` (es/en/pt).

**Acceptance criteria**:
- Accommodations list exposes price range and createdAt range filter controls in the FilterBar
- Events list exposes event start-date range and createdAt range filter controls
- Destinations list exposes createdAt range filter control
- Each filter round-trips: set → URL param set → reload → filter state restored → list narrows correctly
- `pnpm typecheck` passes in apps/admin

tasks.title: Add filter bars to the six previously-unfiltered entity lists
tasks.description:
For each of the six entities with no filter bar today (sponsors, amenities, attractions, event-locations, event-organizers, features), add a `filterBarConfig` to their entity config. For each, also declare the new params on the admin route Zod schema and wire them into the service filter.

**Entities and their filters** (verify against schema before implementing):
- **sponsors**: `isFeatured` (boolean), `includeDeleted` (boolean); add `status` select if sponsor schema has a status enum.
- **amenities**: `includeDeleted` (boolean); add `category`/`group` select if the schema exposes one.
- **attractions**: `includeDeleted` (boolean); add `category` select if present.
- **event-locations**: `includeDeleted` (boolean); add any city/state select available on the schema.
- **event-organizers**: `includeDeleted` (boolean).
- **features**: `includeDeleted` (boolean); add `category` select if present.

**For each entity**, update:
1. `apps/admin/src/features/<entity>/config/<entity>.config.ts` — add `filterBarConfig`.
2. The admin list route's Zod query schema in `packages/schemas/` — declare the new params.
3. The `@repo/service-core` service `adminList` method — apply the filter if the param is present.

**Acceptance criteria**:
- All six lists render a FilterBar after this task
- `includeDeleted=true` returns soft-deleted rows for all six
- `isFeatured` filter (sponsors) narrows correctly
- Each applied filter survives a page reload (URL state persistence)
- Route rejects undeclared params (existing `createAdminListRoute` behavior)
- Config tests: smoke test per list asserting FilterBar presence and at least one filter application

tasks.title: Polish the generic GridCard component
tasks.description:
Improve the visual quality of the generic `GridCard` component: stronger hierarchy, dedicated action affordance, responsive single-column reflow, and a proper empty state.

**Files to modify**:
- `apps/admin/src/components/grid/GridCard.tsx`
- (optionally) the grid container component that renders the empty state

**Polish targets**:
1. **Visual hierarchy**: Entity title should be the most prominent element (font-size, weight). Secondary fields smaller. Avoid flat uniform-weight text blocks.
2. **Action affordance**: Peek / Edit / Delete actions should have a clear, consistently positioned action bar or overlay (e.g. bottom strip or top-right icon cluster). Must be keyboard-accessible (tabIndex, aria-label).
3. **Responsive reflow**: Grid uses CSS grid. On narrow viewports (< `sm`), cards reflow to a single column without horizontal overflow. Use Tailwind `grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3` or equivalent.
4. **Empty state**: When the grid has zero rows, render a styled empty state (icon + message) that visually aligns with the web app design system. Match the pattern used by other empty states in the admin app (check `apps/admin/src/components/` for an existing empty-state component or pattern).
5. **Tailwind v4 only** — no CSS modules.

**Acceptance criteria**:
- Polished generic card renders for any entity config that lacks `renderCard`
- Empty state renders when zero rows are present
- Cards reflow to single column on narrow viewport (test: resize or snapshot)
- Action buttons remain accessible (aria-label, keyboard reachable)
- Existing entity configs are unaffected (backward compatible — no config changes needed)

subtasks.title: Improve visual hierarchy in GridCard (title prominence, field sizing)
Add action affordance (peek/edit/delete action bar or overlay with aria-labels)
Fix responsive reflow (single column on narrow viewport)
Implement grid empty state (icon + message, aligned with admin design)
Write tests: generic card renders for a config, empty state renders at 0 rows, responsive snapshot

tasks.title: Add gridConfig.renderCard and GridCardRenderProps to EntityConfig
tasks.description:
Add the per-entity override mechanism to the entity config type system.

**Files to modify**:
- `apps/admin/src/components/entity-list/types.ts`
- `apps/admin/src/components/grid/GridCard.tsx` (or whichever component resolves which card to render)

**What to implement**:
1. In `types.ts`, add to the existing `gridConfig` sub-shape:
   ```ts
   renderCard?: (props: GridCardRenderProps<TData>) => ReactNode;
   ```
2. Define `GridCardRenderProps<TData>` in the same file:
   ```ts
   export interface GridCardRenderProps<TData> {
     row: TData;
     onPeek: (row: TData) => void;
     onEdit: (row: TData) => void;
     onDelete: (row: TData) => void;
   }
   ```
3. In the grid renderer (wherever `GridCard` is instantiated per row), check:
   - If `gridConfig.renderCard` is defined → call it with `{ row, onPeek, onEdit, onDelete }`.
   - Otherwise → render the polished generic `<GridCard>` (the T-008 version).
4. Add comprehensive JSDoc on `GridCardRenderProps<TData>` and `renderCard`.

**Acceptance criteria**:
- When `renderCard` is absent, polished generic card renders (backward compatible)
- When `renderCard` is present, the custom renderer is called with the correct props
- `GridCardRenderProps<TData>` is exported and usable by external feature config files
- Tests: generic card used when `renderCard` absent; custom renderer called when `renderCard` present
- `pnpm typecheck` passes — no `any` types

subtasks.title: Add renderCard?: (props: GridCardRenderProps<TData>) => ReactNode to gridConfig in types.ts
Define and export GridCardRenderProps<TData> interface with row + 3 action callbacks
Wire renderCard fallback in grid renderer: custom if present, polished generic otherwise
Write tests: generic card default, custom renderer dispatch, action callback signatures

tasks.title: Create grid-only host accommodations config
tasks.description:
Create a new entity config for the host's own accommodations portfolio, separate from the admin accommodations config.

**Files to create**:
- `apps/admin/src/features/accommodations/config/me-accommodations.config.ts`
- `apps/admin/src/features/accommodations/config/me-accommodations.columns.ts` (or reuse columns from the existing accommodations config where sensible)

**Config requirements**:
- `viewConfig.defaultView = 'grid'`
- `viewConfig.allowViewToggle = false` (no table/grid toggle rendered; table mode unreachable)
- `apiConfig` points to the host's own accommodations endpoint (owner-scoped; the same data source the current hand-rolled route shows — `useAccommodationListQuery` scoped by actor)
- Reuse `peekFields` and column definitions from the existing accommodations config where applicable
- A sensible `filterBarConfig` (at minimum `status` select + `includeDeleted` boolean)
- `listItemSchema` sourced from the existing `@repo/schemas` accommodations list item schema

**Acceptance criteria**:
- Config compiles with `pnpm typecheck` — no `any` types
- `defaultView: 'grid'` and `allowViewToggle: false` are set
- The config's `apiConfig` endpoint is owner-scoped (host sees only their own rows)
- Reused columns and peek fields from the admin config do not duplicate logic (import, don't copy)

tasks.title: Full test suite, manual admin smoke, and PR open
tasks.description:
Final integration gate: all tests pass, every touched list is manually verified in the browser, and the PR is opened.

**Automated checks**:
```bash
pnpm --filter hospeda-admin test
pnpm --filter @repo/service-core test
pnpm --filter @repo/schemas test
pnpm typecheck
pnpm lint
```

**Manual admin smoke** (every touched list, in the browser):
- superadmin account: verify all 13 lists have filter bars and operate correctly.
- `number-range` smoke: set a price range on the accommodations list — URL params set, list narrows.
- `date-range` smoke: set a createdAt range — URL params set, list narrows.
- Grid mode on a polished card: verify visual hierarchy, action buttons, responsive reflow.
- HOST account: open `/me/accommodations` — grid-only mode, only own rows, no toggle.
- Post-tags / internal-tags / user-moderation-tags: FilterBar present, includeDeleted works, grid mode available.

**PR**:
- Branch `spec/SPEC-185-admin-entity-lists-v2` → base `staging`
- PR title: `feat(admin): SPEC-185 — entity lists v2 (range filters, grid polish, host portfolio, tags migration)`
- PR body references BETA-75, BETA-72, BETA-76

**Acceptance criteria**:
- All automated test suites exit 0
- Manual smoke covers all 5 FRs

- No `.only()` or hard-coded `.skip()` in committed test code

tasks.title: Post-merge: flip spec + task indexes to completed and close Linear issues
tasks.description:
After the PR is merged:

1. Flip `specs/index.json` entry for SPEC-185: `status: 'completed'`, add `completedAt`, `archived: true`, `archivedAt`, `completionRef` (PR number + merge commit).
2. Flip `tasks/index.json` epic entry for SPEC-185: `status: 'completed'`, `progress: '17/17'`, `archived: true`, `archivedAt`.
3. Update `spec.md` frontmatter: `status: completed`.
4. Close Linear issues BETA-75, BETA-72, BETA-76 as Done, referencing the merged PR.
5. Save engram entry: `topic_key: spec/SPEC-185/completed`, content = final state summary.
6. Commit the index + spec.md changes on a NEW branch (not the spec branch, which is merged) → PR to staging.

**Acceptance criteria**:
- Both index files show SPEC-185 as completed + archived (in sync)
- `spec.md` status = completed
- All 3 Linear issues (BETA-75, BETA-72, BETA-76) closed as Done
- Engram entry saved
- Closeout commit is on a new branch (not the already-merged spec branch)
